title: Survival benchmark definition (right-censored endpoint, nested CV, external cohort)
context: The second benchmark family is survival prediction: overall or progression-free survival with right-censoring. Survival benchmarks have additional leakage risks beyond subtyping — temporal/lookahead leakage (post-baseline or outcome-derived features used as predictors), incorrect censoring handling, and naive metrics that ignore censoring (plain AUC applied to censored outcomes). The benchmark definition must specify the right-censored endpoint and censoring definition, use nested CV to avoid optimistic performance estimates, and declare an independent external cohort. All sources must pass the gate-002 checklist; only aggregate or de-identified individual-level data is in scope.
objective: Create the versioned survival benchmark definition object: specify task type (survival), cancer type, endpoint (OS or PFS) with right-censoring definition cited and confirmed by the domain reviewer, sources (each with gate-002 passage confirmed, aggregate-only where individual-level would be controlled), featureSpec, splitSpec (patient-grouped nested CV with declared independent external cohort, split hashes), preprocessingSpec (fit-on-train-only), and metricSpec (Harrell C-index, Uno C-index, time-dependent AUC, integrated Brier score); emit split-membership files and content hashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint (OS or PFS) and censoring definition are cited to literature and confirmed by the domain reviewer; the censoring handling is explicitly specified (right-censoring, no informative censoring assumed without justification)
Splits are patient/case-grouped with nested cross-validation; a declared independent external cohort from a different study or centre is confirmed as genuinely independent by the domain reviewer

Sources are aggregate-only or publisher-documented de-identified; individual-level survival data requiring a DUA is excluded; all sources have passed the gate-002 checklist with committed gate artifacts

The definition object specifies the metric suite: Harrell C-index, Uno C-index, time-dependent AUC, and integrated Brier score; naive (uncensored) accuracy or AUC is explicitly excluded as a primary metric
Split-membership files and content hashes are emitted and committed; the definition object is versioned and self-contained
Domain reviewer and licence+de-id reviewer sign-offs are recorded alongside the definition object
output: A versioned, committed survival benchmark definition object with right-censored endpoint specification, patient-grouped nested CV splits, declared external cohort, emitted split-membership files and content hashes, and confirmed gate-002 passage for all sources.
